rows.capability_name: Controlled authority gateway transport and callback channel
downstream_phase_implications: Authority integration work must keep provider HTTP mechanics behind the boundary while reusing engine-authored request identity and ingress normalization rules.
Recovery and DR paths must rebuild transport queues from durable engine truth rather than from gateway-local memory.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: letting provider-specific transport logic redefine submission state
mutating legal posture directly from gateway callbacks
treating transport success as proof of legal completion
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may instruct or consume this edge capability through typed contracts, but the edge must not promote payloads to canonical truth or mutate legal state on its own.
rows.notes: The gateway is necessary infrastructure, but the engine still owns intent, request identity, normalization, and legal-state interpretation.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Necessary dependency or transport edge the engine instructs, but not part of the inventive semantic core.

rows.capability_name: Connector worker execution and retry orchestration
downstream_phase_implications: Collection and normalization tasks must document when payloads cross from connector runtime into engine-governed artifacts.
Connector modules should remain thin transport executors behind engine-owned fetch intent and scope controls.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming generic ETL runtime as the inventive center
letting connector retry state decide legal continuation or scope widening
promoting fetched payloads directly into compute without Snapshot formation
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may instruct or consume this edge capability through typed contracts, but the edge must not promote payloads to canonical truth or mutate legal state on its own.
rows.notes: The corpus allows controlled connector use, but it explicitly disclaims generic ETL or simple connector products as the invention.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Necessary dependency or transport edge the engine instructs, but not part of the inventive semantic core.

rows.capability_name: OCR and document-extraction runtime
downstream_phase_implications: Document-ingestion work must preserve a hard handoff from extraction output into engine-owned candidate-fact and Snapshot logic.
Security and privacy controls must quarantine unsafe documents before any retained evidence path is formed.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: allowing OCR confidence to bypass normalization and validation
claiming generic document extraction as the inventive center
treating OCR output as canonical truth
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may instruct or consume this edge capability through typed contracts, but the edge must not promote payloads to canonical truth or mutate legal state on its own.
rows.notes: OCR is an adjacent runtime that supports evidence acquisition but does not itself define engine truth.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Necessary dependency or transport edge the engine instructs, but not part of the inventive semantic core.

rows.capability_name: Transactional ingress checkpoint for callbacks, polls, and imported notices
downstream_phase_implications: Authority ingress pipelines must persist receipt-proof artifacts that later normalization and reconciliation consume.
Restore and replay tooling must rebuild unresolved authority work from persisted ingress receipts, not from broker-local retries.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: normalizing ingress before authenticating the provider channel
recovering ingress correlation from transport timestamps alone
treating duplicate or weakly-bound ingress as safe legal truth
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may instruct or consume this edge capability through typed contracts, but the edge must not promote payloads to canonical truth or mutate legal state on its own.
rows.notes: This checkpoint is outside the inventive core but is the controlled edge the core depends on before it may normalize authority-side signals.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Necessary dependency or transport edge the engine instructs, but not part of the inventive semantic core.

rows.capability_name: Scoped external-data fetch transport
downstream_phase_implications: Connector and import adapters must preserve raw payload identity and provider context without inventing canonical meaning.
Later source-taxonomy work should keep raw-source truth outside the core until normalization is complete.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: equating captured payload bytes with canonical facts
letting fetch transport infer scope or data quality locally
treating external provider availability as equivalent to engine readiness
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may instruct or consume this edge capability through typed contracts, but the edge must not promote payloads to canonical truth or mutate legal state on its own.
rows.notes: External fetch transport is a boundary edge: necessary for intake, but insufficient on its own to create engine truth.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Necessary dependency or transport edge the engine instructs, but not part of the inventive semantic core.

rows.capability_name: User-interface rendering, dashboards, and front-end layout
downstream_phase_implications: Design and shell tasks should treat layout as delivery, not as the source of legal meaning.
Frontend work must preserve engine-authored semantics while remaining free to change layout, styling, and component composition.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming styling, dashboards, or layout as the inventive center
letting visible convenience labels outrank typed reason codes
redefining legal posture through renderer-local copy or badges
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: UI rendering is product embodiment, not the invention as such; only the semantic posture the UI consumes belongs inside the engine.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Staff console, client portal, and admin-console surface embodiment
downstream_phase_implications: Frontend and northbound tasks must model distinct portal, operator, and governance surfaces as consumers of engine-authored semantics.
Route-specific read models should remain replaceable without changing the underlying legal posture vocabulary.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: re-authoring scope, authority, or workflow semantics per surface
treating portal or admin-console state as command-side truth
using console-local session state to bypass engine truth
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: The product includes many surfaces, but none of them becomes the source of manifest, authority, or legal-state truth.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Northbound API gateway, session management, and experience-stream transport
downstream_phase_implications: API and streaming work must preserve command/read separation, stale-view guards, and duplicate suppression at the edge.
Frontend flows must recover via durable receipts and snapshot rebase rather than by replaying writes heuristically.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: promoting ExperienceDelta or receipt transport fields into command truth
treating partial POST success as legal state
using reconnect logic to invent missing transitions client-side
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Northbound delivery is required for the product, but the contract itself says command-side truth remains inside the core engine.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Platform routing, scene restoration, and window choreography
downstream_phase_implications: Desktop and browser shell work must consume route-stable engine posture fields without inventing a second interaction grammar.
Recovery UX should prefer rebase and stable anchors over local recreation of missing state.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: letting local choreography rewrite scope or authority posture
restoring UI selection without the underlying engine-authored stability contract
treating a route alias as a new legal object or scope
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Routing and restoration consume engine-authored shell semantics but do not themselves define what the legal posture means.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Identity, session, step-up, CSRF, and device-binding runtime controls
downstream_phase_implications: Access-control and northbound work must validate every command against live session posture while preserving a separate engine authorization record.
Frontend clients should lean on server-issued session state, not local trust shortcuts, for privileged actions.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: allowing stale or replayed commands to masquerade as valid engine intent
smuggling session runtime controls into engine semantics
treating client-held tokens as the only trust boundary
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Session and command-trust hardening protects the engine but is defined as broader-product runtime control rather than inventive core logic.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Token vault, OAuth session maintenance, and key or secret lifecycle custody
downstream_phase_implications: Authority transport, recovery, and release work must treat secret custody as a separate runtime boundary from engine-owned request identity.
Security automation should preserve token-binding lineage without turning secret storage into command-side truth.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming mere token or OAuth handling as the invention
keeping raw authority tokens in queues, read models, or logs
using secret rotation to silently rebind a request to a different client context
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Provider-specific credential custody mechanics remain outside the core even though the engine depends on them lawfully.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Notification delivery
downstream_phase_implications: Later messaging integrations should read customer-safe projections from the engine and keep delivery state orthogonal.
Workflow and portal modules must treat notification delivery as side-effectful communication, never as the source of truth.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: letting channel failure rewrite underlying engine posture
serializing internal-only reason codes directly into customer messaging
treating a delivered notification as legal or workflow completion
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Notifications communicate engine posture but do not define or complete it.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Durable storage, object store, queue, cache, and observability runtime
downstream_phase_implications: Infrastructure work must keep durable truth rebuildable from manifests, audit evidence, and authority records.
Projection and cache systems should remain disposable and partition-safe, especially across tenant and masking boundaries.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: rebuilding truth from read models instead of durable command-side artifacts
reusing cache entries across tenants or masking postures
treating the queue or cache as the legal system of record
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Runtime topology is essential for operability, but the corpus explicitly keeps legal truth and inventive semantics out of queues, caches, and delivery substrates.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Schema migration, release admission, rollback, backup, restore, DR, and packaging control plane
downstream_phase_implications: Future platform tasks should model fail-forward and restore evidence as runtime governance, not as substitutions for engine lineage.
Release, migration, and recovery work must preserve manifest, audit, privacy, and authority guarantees rather than redefining them.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: blindly replaying live authority mutations during restore or DR
rolling back legal truth by deleting or obscuring persisted evidence
treating release automation as the inventive core
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: Promotion, rollback, restore, and packaging are broader-product control-plane functions that protect the engine without becoming its inventive center.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Standalone advisory forecasting disconnected from manifest, trust, and provenance governance
downstream_phase_implications: Any advisory-only tooling must remain clearly outside filing-capable paths unless it is reintroduced through the governed engine boundary.
Forecast features should either serialize execution-mode boundaries or stay explicitly outside the core decision spine.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming disconnected forecasting as the invention
omitting manifest, trust, or provenance boundaries around analysis products
reusing ungoverned advisory output inside filing-capable flows
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The broader product may render, secure, route, or transport engine-authored artifacts, but it must not infer or mutate core legal posture locally.
rows.notes: The corpus explicitly excludes standalone forecasting models that are disconnected from manifest freeze, trust, and provenance governance.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Broader-product delivery or runtime function that carries or protects engine artifacts without creating their legal meaning.

rows.capability_name: Identity issuance, HMRC sign-up, and agent authorisation setup
downstream_phase_implications: Authority-link and delegation modules must validate imported or existing authority context instead of fabricating it.
User journeys should treat sign-up as an external prerequisite and surface bounded handoff or prerequisite failure posture.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: implementing HMRC sign-up or primary identity issuance inside the engine
treating local tenant policy as equivalent to authority-issued identity
using RPA or local shortcuts to absorb prohibited sign-up journeys
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: Identity proofing, sign-up, and primary authority issuance remain with HMRC or the relevant external provider.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: HMRC-online-services-only tasks and provider-hosted journeys
downstream_phase_implications: Portal and operator journeys must model formal boundary crossings for HMRC-only tasks.
Workflow and posture layers may guide, link, or track completion evidence, but they must not absorb the task semantics.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: presenting guided handoff as if the engine completed the task itself
reimplementing HMRC-only journeys inside product flows
using embedded trusted web surfaces to blur the authority boundary
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: The lawful engine relationship is guidance and handoff, not absorption of HMRC-reserved journeys.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: Authority-owned legal submission truth
downstream_phase_implications: Any state machine that surfaces filing posture must keep authority precedence explicit and typed.
Submission, portal, and governance work must preserve a hard boundary between internal intent and authority-owned truth.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: allowing workflow completion to outrank authority acknowledgement
rewriting authority truth from internal convenience or timeout heuristics
treating internal submitted or done labels as legal truth
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: The engine records and interprets authority truth, but it does not own or replace it.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: HMRC's own calculation service
downstream_phase_implications: Later authority-integration tasks should treat calculation retrieval as comparison input, not as local legal truth creation.
Parity and filing-readiness modules must preserve authority-calculation provenance separately from internal compute results.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming authority calculation retrieval as the inventive center
overwriting authority-returned calculation meaning with local heuristics
presenting internal compute as if it were the same as the authority's own calculation service
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: The engine may request and compare HMRC calculations, but it never becomes HMRC's calculation service.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: Human judgment outside the approved override protocol
downstream_phase_implications: Later collaboration tooling should differentiate off-system context from persisted override or rationale artifacts.
Workflow, override, and audit modules must ingest human judgment only through governed records and typed state transitions.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: claiming generic manual workflow as the invention
treating undocumented human judgment as engine truth
using ad hoc verbal approval to bypass override or audit artifacts
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: Human judgment only enters the engine when it is captured through governed override, workflow, or audit artifacts.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: Reporting-subject legal consent, declaration, and signatory acts
downstream_phase_implications: Approval, portal, and authority-link flows must keep legal consent and delegated authority as explicit prerequisites rather than UI assumptions.
Sign-off journeys must capture and persist evidence of who had the right to act, not just who clicked submit.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: letting step-up completion fabricate a missing signatory basis
treating an internal role assignment as equivalent to external legal consent
using product session state as a substitute for delegated client authority
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: The engine can verify and record signatory or delegation evidence, but it does not create the reporting subject's legal authority.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: External source systems and document providers as raw-source origins
downstream_phase_implications: Collection and normalization work must preserve origin class, source strength, and freshness before promotion into canonical facts.
Future provenance and trust modules should use source origin as input, not as a substitute for canonicalization.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: assuming freshness or correctness because the payload came from an institution
collapsing EvidenceItem, CandidateFact, and CanonicalFact into one raw data bucket
treating source origin as already canonical
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: Banks, document providers, and declared human inputs remain external origins even when the engine later normalizes them.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.

rows.capability_name: Authority-side processing semantics beyond what is returned
downstream_phase_implications: Later authority analytics should preserve unresolved or unknown posture rather than inventing deterministic external state.
Reconciliation and pending-state logic must react only to returned or authenticated authority evidence, not to local optimism.
forbidden_shortcuts_or_false_equivalence: pretending local retries know what the authority is doing internally
reconstructing authority processing semantics from timeout behaviour alone
treating internal queue state as a substitute for authority-side progress
rows.lawful_engine_relationship: The engine may guide, request, compare, record, or react to this function, but it cannot absorb, impersonate, or overwrite the external actor's authority.
rows.notes: The engine can only reason over what the authority returns or lawfully acknowledges; internal provider processing remains external.
rows.why_out_of_scope: Authority-owned, provider-owned, or human-owned behavior outside engine control and not absorbable into the invention boundary.
